title: briza media subsp. elatior
intro: Briza media subsp. elatior, also known as greater quaking grass, is a subspecies of the plant Briza media, belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and soothing digestive problems. Briza media subsp. elatior can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
